title: Architectural Patterns for Hybrid Table Workloads
author: Adam Timm
description: Eight architectural patterns for integrating Hybrid Tables with analytics pipelines, change detection, event-driven architectures, and operational monitoring.

patterns.name: Task-Based Snapshot (MERGE)
patterns.problem: BI dashboards cannot read HT efficiently (COLUMN_BASED scan, no result cache)
patterns.solution: Scheduled Task runs MERGE from HT into standard table; BI reads the standard table

patterns.name: Watermark-Based Change Detection
patterns.problem: Streams not supported on HT; need incremental change detection
patterns.solution: Track last_sync_ts in a watermark table; filter MERGE source by updated_at > watermark
patterns.requirement: Application must set updated_at on every UPDATE

patterns.name: Event-Driven CDC (External Notifications)
patterns.problem: External systems need real-time event delivery from HT changes
patterns.solution: Task polls for changes + Notification Integration publishes to SNS/PubSub/Webhook

patterns.name: Dynamic Tables on Standard Table Copy
patterns.problem: DTs cannot read from HT directly
patterns.solution: HT -> Task -> Standard Table -> Dynamic Table for derived datasets

patterns.name: Materialized Views on Standard Table Copy
patterns.problem: MVs cannot be created on HT
patterns.solution: Create MV on the standard table mirror for precomputed aggregations

patterns.name: Fan-In Aggregation
patterns.problem: Analytics needs to join across multiple HTs from different domains
patterns.solution: Task joins multiple HTs into a single denormalized standard table

patterns.name: Hot/Cold Data Tiering
patterns.problem: HT storage grows unbounded; old data accessed infrequently
patterns.solution: Scheduled Task archives rows older than N days to standard table; DELETE from HT
patterns.storage_note: HT has 2 TB per-database quota; tiering prevents hitting this limit

patterns.name: Alert-Based Monitoring
patterns.problem: Sub-second HT queries not in QUERY_HISTORY; throttling invisible without monitoring
patterns.solution: Query AGGREGATE_QUERY_HISTORY for latency/throttling; CREATE ALERT for automated notification

hybrid_table_limitations_addressed: Dynamic Tables cannot read from HT
Materialized Views cannot be created on HT
Clustering keys not supported (indexes used instead)
AGGREGATE_QUERY_HISTORY needed for sub-second query monitoring

key_sql_patterns.merge_ht_to_st: MERGE INTO standard_table USING hybrid_table ON pk = pk WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT
key_sql_patterns.watermark_filter: WHERE updated_at > (SELECT last_sync_ts FROM watermarks WHERE table_name = 'orders')
key_sql_patterns.task_schedule: CREATE TASK ... SCHEDULE = '5 MINUTES' AS MERGE INTO ...
key_sql_patterns.dt_on_st: CREATE DYNAMIC TABLE ... TARGET_LAG = '10 MINUTES' AS SELECT ... FROM standard_table_copy
key_sql_patterns.mv_on_st: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW ... AS SELECT ... FROM standard_table_copy
key_sql_patterns.archive_delete: INSERT INTO archive SELECT * FROM ht WHERE created_at < cutoff; DELETE FROM ht WHERE created_at < cutoff;
key_sql_patterns.alert_throttle: CREATE ALERT ... IF(EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM AGGREGATE_QUERY_HISTORY WHERE throttled > threshold)) THEN notify

reference_architecture: Application -> Hybrid Table (OLTP) -> Task (MERGE/snapshot) -> Standard Table (analytics) -> [DT | MV | BI Tools]

related_guides.title: Converting Standard Tables to Hybrid Tables
